title: Gin’s Gratitude Reveals Sanji’s Kindness
description: On pages 12–15, Sanji feeds Gin despite the danger, revealing that his compassion for the hungry matters more to him than restaurant rules or pirate labels.
scene: Gin arrives broken and starving, while the Baratie cooks judge him as an enemy; Sanji alone treats hunger as something that must be answered.

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: My name's gin. I'm a member of the KrieG pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So you said you're a pirate? Can I ask what your objective is?

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: krig pirate crew "fighter" GIN

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm going to find one piece,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Which is why I'll go to grand line!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If you're still looking for a cook, I'm assuming you don't have very many people on your crew yet, right...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: with him, that makes S!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why're you counting me in!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You don't seem to be a bad guy so let me give you a piece of advice...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Give up,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: on going to grand line.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You're still young. No need to recklessly rush into things.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: besides, the grand line is just one part of all the oceans in this world.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If you want to be a pirate, there's plenty of other seas for you to roam out there.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Do you happen to know something about Grand Line?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: No... I don't... I don't know anything...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That's why it's so terrifying...!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: For a member of the KrieG pirates, you sure are acting like a big scaredy cat.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What's KrieG?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Barate's kitchen

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The hors d'oeuvre for table 3 is ready!

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: Then take it already, you bastard! We ain't got no waiters!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But you sure that was alright?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That dude you whaled on earlier is a member of the Krig Pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Yeah, I recall hearing something about that.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Don KrieG's the strongest and baddest pirate to roam this part of the ocean!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He's a pirate fleet Admiral who commands so ships!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He's a monster!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So what?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I heard the KrieG pirates were over 5000 men strong... so if that guy that you beat up...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: were to go and tell Don Krieg what happened to him here,

summary: This part reveals the core of Sanji’s morality: no matter who a person is, he cannot ignore someone starving in front of him.
